status_notes: meti.go.jp returned HTTP 403 to every automated fetch attempted during research (2026-07-16), including plain HTML pages, across /english/press/, /policy/, and /shingikai/ paths — appears to be domain-wide bot/WAF protection; pages are publicly indexed and human-browsable.
official_name: Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry, Japan
notes: See also registry/regulation/jp/go/meti.json (Unfair Competition Prevention Act, Installment Sales Act) for the regulation-type file under this same namespace.

match_nodes.description: Cybersecurity Management Guidelines — executive/CISO-level cybersecurity governance guidance

data.notes: Jointly issued by METI and IPA. First issued 2015; current Ver. 3.0 issued March 2023. Built around 3 principles management must recognize (own-initiative promotion; supply-chain-wide attention; proactive stakeholder communication) and 10 important items CISOs should direct (risk-management system, resourcing, risk identification/response planning, supply-chain security, incident response, stakeholder communication). Japan's flagship executive-level cybersecurity governance framework, widely cited alongside NIST CSF and ISO 27001 in Japanese compliance work.

urls.note: English version, titled 'for Japanese Enterprise Executives'. Blocked (HTTP 403) to automated fetch as of 2026-07-16; freely available to human browsers, not paywalled.

match_nodes.description: AI Guidelines for Business — Japan's central soft-law AI governance instrument

data.notes: Jointly issued by METI and MIC. Ver 1.0 released April 19, 2024; updated to Ver 1.1 around March 2025. Organized around 10 cross-sector guiding principles (human-centricity, safety, fairness, privacy, security, transparency, accountability, education/literacy, fair competition, innovation) in a foundational-values/principles/practical-tools three-tier structure, consolidating the 2019 Cabinet Office Social Principles of Human-Centric AI, 2021 METI AI Governance Guidelines, and 2019 MIC AI R&D/Utilisation Guidelines. Sets expectations for AI developer/provider/user security and safety practices.

urls.note: Ver 1.1 provisional English translation. Blocked (HTTP 403) to automated fetch as of 2026-07-16; freely available to human browsers.

match_nodes.description: Toward Building Partnerships with Business Partners to Enhance Cybersecurity Across the Entire Supply Chain

data.official_name: Toward Building Partnerships with Business Partners to Enhance Cybersecurity Across the Entire Supply Chain
data.notes: Jointly issued by METI and the Japan Fair Trade Commission (JFTC), October 28, 2022; supplemented December 2025 with additional case-based Q&A guidance. Addresses how ordering companies can request/support suppliers' cybersecurity measures without violating the Antimonopoly Act or Subcontract Act — flags three risk areas: unilateral pricing, cost-burden requests for security measures, and forced purchase/use of specific tools. Governs the legal/contractual mechanics of supply-chain cybersecurity requirements between large enterprises and SME suppliers. Japanese-only; no English version located.

urls.note: Original document (Japanese only). JFTC hosting was not confirmed blocked; meti.go.jp mirror returned HTTP 403.

match_nodes.description: Security Measures Evaluation System for Supply Chain Strengthening — forthcoming tiered supply-chain security certification

data.official_name: Security Measures Evaluation System for Strengthening Supply Chains
data.notes: METI, jointly with the Cabinet Secretariat's National Cybersecurity Office. Timeline: interim report April 14, 2025 -> draft 'System Construction Policy' for public comment December 26, 2025 -> finalized System Construction Policy published March 27, 2026. Tiered star-3/star-4/star-5 assessment based on supply-chain importance/connectivity, self-assessment (with expert verification) or third-party evaluation; explicitly targets IT infrastructure including cloud environments. Full operational launch now targeted around end of FY2026 (Jan-Mar 2027); FY2026 used for demonstration projects and platform build-out. Designed to complement SECURITY ACTION (star-1/star-2, see registry/control/jp/go/ipa.json), JAMA/JAPIA automotive cybersecurity guidelines, and ISMS certification. Status: not yet operational — revisit once finalized assessment criteria are published.

urls.note: Overview page. Blocked (HTTP 403) to automated fetch as of 2026-07-16; freely available to human browsers.
English interim-report coverage (April 2025) — the finalized March 2026 construction policy has no English coverage yet.
